system.description: 3 laptops on same WiFi router - controller, AP agent, monitor+attacker

uplink.description: Home WiFi uplink for internet access and inter-laptop communication (if multi-device testing)

ap.description: Virtual AP created on same laptop (hostapd)

dhcp.description: DHCP server assigns IPs to phones connecting to AP

nat.description: Optional NAT: forward AP clients (192.168.88.0/24) to internet via wlo1 (10.12.x.x)

controller.role_description: SDN controller listens on AP interface to manage AP metrics and decisions

monitor.role_description: Monitor/jammer agent on same laptop, acts as aggressive traffic source

phones.description: Real WiFi phones connecting via hostapd AP

phone_1.name: Phone1
phone_1.description: Runs iperf3 -s (server) listening for throughput measurements

phone_2.name: Phone2
phone_2.description: Second phone for redundancy or dual throughput tests (optional)

phase_1_setup.description: Start controller, AP, DHCP, monitor agents

phase_2_baseline.description: Collect metrics on clean network (no jammer)

phase_3_jammer_active.description: UDP packet flood active, measure degradation

phase_4_detection.description: Controller detects jammer and triggers response

jammer.description: UDP flood from Monitor/Attacker laptop (10.135.40.120) to broadcast, detected by Controller (10.135.40.9)

actions.name: CHANNEL_SWITCH
actions.description: Switch from channel 6 to 11 to escape jammer

throughput.description: Measure TCP throughput from controller to phone

packet_rate.description: Packets per second on AP interface

deprecated_fields_removed.refactor_notes: Simplified to single laptop running all roles. All components communicate via local socket or loopback (127.0.0.1) or AP interface (192.168.88.1). No remote TCP/UDP communication needed.

single_laptop_roles: Role 1: SDN Controller - Listens on 192.168.88.1:9000, implements detection logic, sends commands to hostapd
Role 2: Access Point - hostapd on ap0, SSID 'SDN-TestNet', gateway 192.168.88.1
Role 3: DHCP Server - dnsmasq assigns IPs from 192.168.88.10-200 to phones
Role 4: Monitor Agent - Measures throughput/latency, generates jammer traffic
Role 5: Jammer Detection - Monitors metrics, triggers response when conditions met
architecture_notes.logical_separation: Even though physically single machine, roles are logically distinct (controller logic, AP management, monitoring). Can be split to separate machines later by changing IP config.

communication_flow.controller_to_ap: localhost socket or hostapd_cli commands (no network latency)
communication_flow.phones_to_internet: NAT through wlo1 (optional, for real internet access)
